target.name: Lepadiformine B and C
target.motivation: Lepadiformine B (1b) and C (1c) are tricyclic alkaloids that possess a tricyclic perhydropyrido[2,1-j]-quinolone structure bearing an n-butyl group at the C2 position. They show moderate inhibition of the cardiac inward rectifier K+ channel and moderate cytotoxicity against several tumor cell lines. The interesting structure and biological activities have prompted chemists to develop various intellectual and elegant strategies.

prior_syntheses.key_strategy: N-Boc alpha-amino nitriles as trianion synthons

success_criteria.free_text: Total syntheses of (±)-lepadiformine B and C, with improved efficiency and scalability compared to previous methods.

key_disconnections.reasoning: The hydroxymethyl group at C13 can be introduced by a one-carbon elongation of tertiary lactam 2 through Dixon’s reductive cyanation and subsequent transformations.
A Dieckmann-type condensation between the angular nitrile with the acetyl group in cis-acetamide 3 can generate the pyrrolidine C ring of lactam 2.

Conversion to cis-acetamide 3 from the known Cbz-protected cis-2-allyl-8a-cyanodecahydroquinoline (4) involves relative configuration retention. This cyclization approach may save manipulations for the construction of the B ring and the side chain and provide divergent syntheses for both cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ.
The sterically well-defined alpha-aminonitrile 5 can be obtained by a Strecker reaction of the alpha-monosubstituted cyclohexanone derivative 6.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The overall strategy involves building the tricyclic core from a cyclohexanone derivative. The key steps are a stereodivergent deprotection-initiated alkylative/reductive cyclization to form the decahydroquinoline system, followed by a Dieckmann-type condensation to form the pyrrolidine ring. The C13 hydroxymethyl group is introduced late in the synthesis via reductive cyanation and subsequent transformations.

thought.background: The synthesis commences with the preparation of 6 through the alpha-alkylation of cyclohexanone. Kita's protocol is a known method for alpha-alkylation.
thought.gap: Optimal conditions for the alpha-alkylation need to be determined.
thought.decision: To prepare 6a, chose Kita's protocol using 1-bromo-3,3-ethylenedioxypropane (7a) with cyclohexanone and LiHMDS in DMF at -50 °C, expecting the desired product 6a in good yield.

outcome.observation: Treatment of bromide 7a with 3 equiv of cyclohexanone and LiHMDS in DMF at -50 °C proceeded smoothly to give 81% yield of the desired product 6a.

evidence.text_span: Treatment of bromide 7a with 3 equiv of cyclohexanone and LiHMDS in DMF at −50 °C proceeded smoothly to give 81% yield of the desired product 6a (Scheme 2).
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: The alpha-alkylation conditions were optimized for 6a. The analogue 6b is also needed.
thought.gap: Apply the optimized conditions to synthesize 6b.
thought.decision: To prepare 6b, applied the same conditions as for 6a using bromide 7b, expecting a similar good yield.

outcome.observation: The analogue 6b was also achieved in 72% isolated yield with bromide 7b.

evidence.text_span: The analogue 6b was also achieved in 72% isolated yield with bromide 7b.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: With 6a in hand, the next step is to form the alpha-aminonitrile 5a.
thought.gap: Perform the Strecker reaction and Cbz protection to obtain 5a.
thought.decision: To obtain 5a, chose a Strecker reaction of 6a with KCN in ammonia at room temperature, followed by CbzCl protection, expecting a single alpha-aminonitrile product.

outcome.observation: The subsequent Strecker reaction of 6a, i.e., with KCN in ammonia at room temperature, and CbzCl protection proceeded readily to deliver single alpha-aminonitrile 5a in 91% yield.

evidence.text_span: The subsequent Strecker reaction of 6a, i.e., with KCN in ammonia at room temperature, and CbzCl protection proceeded readily to deliver single α-aminonitrile 5a in 91% yield.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: The Strecker reaction and Cbz protection worked well for 6a to give 5a.
thought.gap: Apply the same tactic to synthesize 5b from 6b.
thought.decision: To obtain 5b, employed the same tactic as for 5a, expecting a similar good yield.

outcome.observation: This tactic was employed to obtain 5b in 84% yield.

evidence.text_span: This tactic was employed to obtain 5b in 84% yield.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: The next key step is the deprotectively reductive cyclization to form the 8a-CDHQ system.
thought.gap: Investigate conditions for the deprotectively reductive cyclization of 5a.
thought.decision: To achieve cyclization of 5a, chose treatment with 1.0 equiv of BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane, expecting the cyclized product 8a in excellent yield.

outcome.observation: we found that the treatment of α-aminonitrile 5a with 1.0 equiv of BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane afforded the cyclized product 8a in excellent yield.

evidence.text_span: we found that the treatment of α-aminonitrile 5a with 1.0 equiv of BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane afforded the cyclized product 8a in excellent yield.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: The deprotectively reductive cyclization worked for 5a. Now to apply it to 5b.
thought.gap: Apply the same conditions to 5b and observe the outcome.
thought.decision: To cyclize 5b, applied the same conditions (1.0 equiv BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane) as for 5a, expecting the cyclized product 8b.

outcome.observation: The application of the conditions on aminonitrile 5b did not yield the expected cyclized product, but a Cbz-free uncyclized product, which was then subjected to protection with CbzCl back to the original 5b.

evidence.text_span: The application of the conditions on aminonitrile 5b did not yield the expected cyclized product, but a Cbz-free uncyclized product, which was then subjected to protection with CbzCl back to the original 5b.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: The initial attempt to cyclize 5b resulted in Cbz deprotection without cyclization.
thought.gap: Modify the conditions to achieve cyclization of 5b, potentially by increasing the Lewis acid amount.
thought.decision: To achieve cyclization of 5b, treated it with 1.3 equiv of BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane, expecting cyclization and then Cbz protection to get 8b.

outcome.observation: Treatment with 1.3 equiv of BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane resulted the formation of a Cbz-free cyclized product, which was protected with CbzCl to produce the cyclized product 8b in 33% yield.

evidence.text_span: Treatment with 1.3 equiv of BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane resulted the formation of a Cbz-free cyclized product, which was protected with CbzCl to produce the cyclized product 8b in 33% yield.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: The cyclized product 8b was obtained, but its NMR spectra were not identical to the known Cbz-protected cis-2-butyl-8a-CDHQ.
thought.gap: Verify the stereochemistry of 8b, which is suspected to be trans.
thought.decision: To verify the stereochemistry, transformed the Cbz group to an acetyl group via HBr/AcOH deprotection and subsequent acetylation, then compared the spectra to known trans-N-acetyl-2-n-butyl-8a-CDHQ (9).

outcome.observation: The spectra of the resulting product were consistent with those of known trans-N-acetyl-2-n-butyl-8a-CDHQ (9). Hence, the results confirm that the deprotectively reductive cyclization of 5b affords the product with the trans configuration rather than the desired cis configuration.

evidence.text_span: To verify the supposition, the Cbz group was transformed to an acetyl group using the protocol of HBr/AcOH deprotection and then acetylation. The spectra of the resulting product were consistent with those of known trans-N-acetyl-2-n-butyl-8a-CDHQ (9). Hence, the results confirm that the deprotectively reductive cyclization of 5b affords the product with the trans configuration rather than the desired cis configuration.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: The previous attempt to cyclize 5b to 8b gave a low yield (33%) and required an extra Cbz protection step. The product was confirmed to be trans-configured.
thought.gap: Improve the yield and conciseness of the synthesis of trans-9 from 5b.
thought.decision: To improve the yield and conciseness, directly treated 5b with 2 equiv BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane followed by acetylation, aiming for a higher yield of trans-9.

outcome.observation: Even so, as a crucial intermediate for the synthesis of fasicularin, the yield of the trans acetyl product 9 was improved up to 81% through direct treatment of 5b with 2 equiv BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane followed by acetylation.

evidence.text_span: Even so, as a crucial intermediate for the synthesis of fasicularin, the yield of the trans acetyl product 9 was improved up to 81% through direct treatment of 5b with 2 equiv BF3·OEt2 and triethylsilane followed by acetylation.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: Deprotectively reductive cyclization of ketal 5b affords the trans product. It is inferred that deprotectively allylative cyclization of acetal 5a will result in the formation of the cis product.
thought.gap: Perform the deprotection-initiated allylative cyclization of 5a.
thought.decision: To obtain the cis product, treated 5a with BF3·OEt2 and allyltrimethylsilane, expecting a deprotection-initiated allylative cyclization to cis-2-allyl-8a-CDHQ (4).

outcome.observation: Thus, treatment of 5a with BF3·OEt2 and allyltrimethylsilane triggered a deprotection-initiated allylative cyclization and resulted in an inseparable mixture of the desired cis-2-allyl-8a-CDHQ (4) and enecarbamate 10 (~6.6:1) in 87% combined yield.

evidence.text_span: Thus, treatment of 5a with BF3·OEt2 and allyltrimethylsilane triggered a deprotection-initiated allylative cyclization and resulted in an inseparable mixture of the desired cis-2-allyl-8a-CDHQ (4) and enecarbamate 10 (~6.6:1) in 87% combined yield (for optimization of the deprotection-initiated allylative cyclization, see Supporting Information).
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: An inseparable mixture of 4 and 10 was obtained from the previous step.
thought.gap: Separate the desired product 4 and convert it to primary alcohol 11.
thought.decision: To obtain primary alcohol 11, subjected the mixture to Sia2BH-mediated hydroboration/oxidation, expecting separation and conversion.

outcome.observation: Subjecting the mixture to Sia2BH-mediated hydroboration/oxidation could yield primary alcohol 11 in 78% yield and enecarbamate 10 in 16% yield.

evidence.text_span: Subjecting the mixture to Sia2BH-mediated hydroboration/oxidation could yield primary alcohol 11 in 78% yield and enecarbamate 10 in 16% yield.
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: Primary alcohol 11 was obtained, and its spectral data were consistent with previously published values. However, further confirmation of the cis configuration is desired.
thought.gap: Unequivocally establish the cis configuration of 11.
thought.decision: To confirm the cis configuration, treated primary alcohol 11 with TsCl followed by substitution with LiBr to obtain crystalline bromide 12, then performed X-ray analysis.

outcome.observation: Moreover, to our delight, simple treatment of primary alcohol 11 with TsCl followed by substitution with LiBr gave a yield of 84% of crystalline bromide 12, whose structure was established unequivocally by X-ray analysis (CCDC No. 1939819), corroborating the present cis configuration in primary alcohol 11.

evidence.text_span: Moreover, to our delight, simple treatment of primary alcohol 11 with TsCl followed by substitution with LiBr gave a yield of 84% of crystalline bromide 12, whose structure was established unequivocally by X-ray analysis (CCDC No. 1939819), corroborating the present cis configuration in primary alcohol 11 (Scheme 2).
evidence.section: Improved Synthesis toward cis- and trans-8a-CDHQ 4 and 9

thought.background: With alcohol 11 in hand, the n-butyl appendage needs to be formed.
thought.gap: Convert alcohol 11 to aldehyde 13.
thought.decision: To convert 11 to 13, chose Parikh-Doering oxidation conditions, expecting a good yield.

outcome.observation: the Parikh−Doering oxidation to aldehyde 13 in 66% yield

evidence.text_span: With alcohol 11 in hand, the n-butyl appendage could be achieved according to a three-step protocol (Scheme 4): the Parikh−Doering oxidation to aldehyde 13 in 66% yield,
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: Aldehyde 13 was obtained.
thought.gap: Convert aldehyde 13 to olefin 14.
thought.decision: To convert 13 to 14, chose Wittig olefination with Ph3P=CH2, expecting good yield.

outcome.observation: the Wittig olefination with Ph3P=CH2 to olefin 14 in 85% yield

evidence.text_span: the Wittig olefination with Ph3P=CH2 to olefin 14 in 85% yield,
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: Olefin 14 was obtained.
thought.gap: Saturate the alkene portion of 14 to form 15 without removing the Cbz group.
thought.decision: To saturate the alkene, chose flavin-mediated aerobic hydrogenation conditions, expecting selective hydrogenation without Cbz deprotection.

outcome.observation: and flavin-mediated aerobic hydrogenation conditions to 15 in 99% yield. Aerobic hydrogenation conditions only effect saturation of the alkene portion but do not remove the Cbz group, which avoids the double consecutive epimerization initiated by Pd-catalyzed hydrogenation conditions.

evidence.text_span: and flavin-mediated aerobic hydrogenation conditions to 15 in 99% yield.15 Aerobic hydrogenation conditions only effect saturation of the alkene portion but do not remove the Cbz group, which avoids the double consecutive epimerization initiated by Pd-catalyzed hydrogenation conditions.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: Compound 15 was obtained with the n-butyl appendage and Cbz protection.
thought.gap: Remove the Cbz group and introduce an acetyl group to form acetamide 3.
thought.decision: To convert 15 to 3, removed the Cbz portion with HBr in acetic acid followed by acetylation, expecting a good overall yield.

outcome.observation: The Cbz portion of 15 was removed within HBr in acetic acid followed by acetylation to furnish acetamide 3 in 71% overall yield.

evidence.text_span: The Cbz portion of 15 was removed within HBr in acetic acid followed by acetylation to furnish acetamide 3 in 71% overall yield.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: Acetamide 3 was obtained.
thought.gap: Form the tricyclic ketone 16 via Dieckmann-type condensation.
thought.decision: To form 16, treated acetamide 3 with LiHMDS followed by acidic workup, expecting the tricyclic ketone.

outcome.observation: Treatment of acetamide 3 with LiHMDS followed by acidic workup afforded tricyclic ketone 16 in 77% yield.

evidence.text_span: Treatment of acetamide 3 with LiHMDS followed by acidic workup afforded tricyclic ketone 16 in 77% yield.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: Tricyclic ketone 16 was obtained.
thought.gap: Convert ketone 16 to conjugated lactam 17 through reduction, mesylation, and elimination.
thought.decision: To form 17, reduced the ketone group of 16 with NaBH4, mesylated the resulting alcohol, and then eliminated it with DBU, expecting the conjugated lactam.

outcome.observation: After the reduction of the ketone group of 16 with NaBH4, the resulting alcohol was mesylated and then eliminated by DBU to furnish conjugated lactam 17 in 87% yield over three steps.

evidence.text_span: After the reduction of the ketone group of 16 with NaBH4, the resulting alcohol was mesylated and then eliminated by DBU to furnish conjugated lactam 17 in 87% yield over three steps.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: Conjugated lactam 17 was obtained.
thought.gap: Saturate the olefin to form tricyclic lactam 2.
thought.decision: To form 2, performed catalytic hydrogenation at ambient pressure, expecting saturation of the olefin.

outcome.observation: Catalytic hydrogenation at ambient pressure saturated the olefin to tricyclic lactam 2 in 90% yield.

evidence.text_span: Catalytic hydrogenation at ambient pressure saturated the olefin to tricyclic lactam 2 in 90% yield.
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: Tricyclic lactam 2 was obtained.
thought.gap: Reduce lactam 2 to lepadiformine C (1c).
thought.decision: To synthesize 1c, performed LiAlH4 reduction of lactam 2, expecting the final product.

outcome.observation: LiAlH4 reduction of lactam 2 afforded a 76% yield of lepadiformine C (1c) whose spectral properties were in accordance with the reported data.

evidence.text_span: LiAlH4 reduction of lactam 2 afforded a 76% yield of lepadiformine C (1c) whose spectral properties were in accordance with the reported data.6l
evidence.section: Synthesis of Lepadiformine C (1c)

thought.background: With access to the key tricyclic lactam 2, the introduction of a substituent on the gamma-lactam could be addressed through Dixon’s Ir-catalyzed hydrosilylation−cyanation protocol.
thought.gap: Perform the reductive cyanation of lactam 2 to aminonitrile 18.
thought.decision: To form 18, subjected lactam 2 to Dixon's Ir-catalyzed hydrosilylation-cyanation protocol, expecting the aminonitrile.

outcome.observation: Lactam 2 was able to undergo reductive cyanation to provide aminonitrile 18, as demonstrated by our successful transformations in the synthesis of fasicularin (I to II, eq 1; Scheme 5).

evidence.text_span: Lactam 2 was able to undergo reductive cyanation to provide aminonitrile 18, as demonstrated by our successful transformations in the synthesis of fasicularin (I to II, eq 1; Scheme 5).
evidence.section: Conversion to Lepadiformine (1b) and Its Analogues

thought.background: Crude tricyclic cis-alpha-aminonitrile 18 was obtained, but it appeared labile and easily oxidized back to lactam 2, especially after column chromatography or longer standing.
thought.gap: Directly convert the crude aminonitrile 18 to alpha-substituted amines without purification.
thought.decision: To avoid decomposition, envisioned converting crude 18 to alpha-substituted amines through the Bruylants reaction with Grignard reagents, starting with ethynylmagnesium chloride.

outcome.observation: Thus, direct treatment of the crude aminonitrile 18 with Grignard reagents, ethynylmagnesium chloride (HC≡CMgCl)), and trimethylsilylmethylmagnesium chloride (TMSCH2MgCl)) afforded the alpha-substituted amines adducts 19 in 34% yield and 20 in 57% yield.

evidence.text_span: Thus, direct treatment of the crude aminonitrile 18 with Grignard reagents, ethynylmagnesium chloride (HC≡CMgCl)), and trimethylsilylmethylmagnesium chloride (TMSCH2MgCl)) afforded the α-substituted amines adducts 19 in 34% yield and 20 in 57% yield.
evidence.section: Conversion to Lepadiformine (1b) and Its Analogues

thought.background: Crude tricyclic cis-alpha-aminonitrile 18 was obtained and successfully converted to 19.
thought.gap: Convert crude 18 to another alpha-substituted amine, 20, using a different Grignard reagent.
thought.decision: To form 20, directly treated crude aminonitrile 18 with trimethylsilylmethylmagnesium chloride, expecting the alpha-substituted amine adduct.

outcome.observation: Thus, direct treatment of the crude aminonitrile 18 with Grignard reagents, ethynylmagnesium chloride (HC≡CMgCl)), and trimethylsilylmethylmagnesium chloride (TMSCH2MgCl)) afforded the alpha-substituted amines adducts 19 in 34% yield and 20 in 57% yield.

evidence.text_span: Thus, direct treatment of the crude aminonitrile 18 with Grignard reagents, ethynylmagnesium chloride (HC≡CMgCl)), and trimethylsilylmethylmagnesium chloride (TMSCH2MgCl)) afforded the α-substituted amines adducts 19 in 34% yield and 20 in 57% yield.
evidence.section: Conversion to Lepadiformine (1b) and Its Analogues

thought.background: The goal is to hydrolyze alpha-amino nitrile 18 to the corresponding acid or carboxyl derivative.
thought.gap: Find suitable hydrolysis conditions for 18.
thought.decision: To hydrolyze 18, examined a number of hydrolysis conditions including acid, basic, or transition-metal-mediated or even under sealed or microwave-assisted forced conditions, expecting the desired products.

outcome.observation: Subsequent hydrolysis of alpha-amino nitrile 18 to the corresponding acid or carboxyl derivative turned out to be more challenging than expected. A number of hydrolysis conditions including acid, basic, or transition-metal-mediated or even under sealed or microwave-assisted forced conditions were examined (see Supporting Information), but none of these conditions could provide the desired products.

evidence.text_span: Subsequent hydrolysis of α-amino nitrile 18 to the corresponding acid or carboxyl derivative turned out to be more challenging than expected. A number of hydrolysis conditions including acid, basic, or transition-metal-mediated or even under sealed or microwave-assisted forced conditions were examined (see Supporting Information), but none of these conditions could provide the desired products.
evidence.section: Conversion to Lepadiformine (1b) and Its Analogues

thought.background: Previous hydrolysis attempts of 18 failed.
thought.gap: Find effective conditions to convert 18 to a carboxyl derivative, specifically methyl ester 21.
thought.decision: To form methyl ester 21, found that Rychnovsky’s conditions of acidic methanolysis were effective, expecting the desired product from tricyclic lactam 2.

outcome.observation: Eventually, we found that Rychnovsky’s conditions of acidic methanolysis5j were effective to form methyl ester 21 in 33% overall yield from tricyclic lactam 2.

evidence.text_span: Eventually, we found that Rychnovsky’s conditions of acidic methanolysis5j were effective to form methyl ester 21 in 33% overall yield from tricyclic lactam 2.
evidence.section: Conversion to Lepadiformine (1b) and Its Analogues

thought.background: Methyl ester 21 was obtained.
thought.gap: Complete the synthesis by reducing methyl ester 21 to alcohol 1b (Lepadiformine B).
thought.decision: To synthesize 1b, performed LiAlH4 reduction of methyl ester 21, expecting the final product.

outcome.observation: Completion of the synthesis was accomplished by LiAlH4 reduction of methyl ester 21 to produce alcohol 1b in 90% yield, whose spectral data were in accordance with the reported values of lepadiformine B.

evidence.text_span: Completion of the synthesis was accomplished by LiAlH4 reduction of methyl ester 21 to produce alcohol 1b in 90% yield, whose spectral data were in accordance with the reported values of lepadiformine B.6l
evidence.section: Conversion to Lepadiformine (1b) and Its Analogues

validation.comparison_to_prior: The methodology facilitates the rapid and efficient assembly of two 2-alkyl-8a-CDHQ systems for the total synthesis of marine tricyclic alkaloids lepadiformine and fasicularin. Cbz-protected cis-8a-2-allylCDHQ (4) can be achieved in 3 steps with 64% yield (compared to 37% yield over 5 steps previously). Acetyl-protected trans-8a-2-n-butylCDHQ (9) can be achieved in 4 steps with 49% yield (compared to 20% yield over 9 steps previously).
validation.significance: The developed protocol is efficient and readily scalable, not requiring harsh conditions. It provides a new approach toward the ascidian family alkaloids and allows for the synthesis of various lepadiformine B analogues.

source.title: Total Syntheses of (±)-Lepadiformine B and C.
source.authors: Hsiao WT, Wu JL, Chiou WH.
